description:
Cleanup ticket — file now, do after 22.12 lands.

fn_constants (HashMap<Var, FnId> per spec, src/ir_typer.rs) was introduced as a parallel mechanism to track \"this Var is exactly fn F\" because the type system couldn't express it. closure_lit (22.8) is exactly that expression. The two mechanisms now overlap.

## Scope

1. Audit every reader of fn_constants:
   - rewrite_known_target_closures (src/ir_typer.rs:860+)
   - phase-1 walker's CallClosure-with-known-target branch (line ~725)
   - cont_slot0_descr's CallClosure branch (line ~779)
   - codegen chain_repr's resolve_sid_under path
   - any others surfaced by grep

2. For each, replace fn_constants[v] lookup with closure_lit extraction from env.get(v). Verify equivalence on every fixture.

3. If equivalence holds across the test suite, delete fn_constants from FnTypes, delete callsite_fn_consts propagation, simplify rewrite_known_target_closures or delete it (if codegen-direct in 22.11 fully covers the rewrite intent).

4. If equivalence does NOT hold for some reader (rare edge case where fn_constants tracks something singletons don't), document the gap and keep fn_constants as a narrow shim — but never as a parallel general mechanism.

## Acceptance

- cargo test --workspace green.
- src/ir_typer.rs no longer carries an unmotivated parallel map. Either fn_constants is gone, or its remaining scope is documented in code.
- Lines of code net negative.

## BLOCKED BY

- fz-ul4.27.22.12 [[fz-cps-seam.force-drop]]
title: fz-cps-seam.retire-fn-constants: retire parallel fn_constants once closure_lit subsumes it
